memory.summary: I've got a free afternoon, but the project is barely started and due Thursday. Instead of doing the quick chore, I'm leaning toward leisure now since I value relationships more than this task right now.
That quick chore win feels hollow; I'd rather hang out with my friends now since the project deadline isn't until Thursday.

I spent an hour on a trivial chore for instant validation, but the real project is still untouched while my afternoon slips away. My friends are waiting, and I feel guilty about prioritizing them over this looming deadline.
That instant win from the trivial chore feels like a cheap distraction while my real project rots; I'm trading actual progress for fleeting validation again.
Another instant win on a trivial chore while the real project rots; I'm just trading actual progress for fleeting validation again.
I just got another quick win on this tiny chore, but it's just more procrastination while my real project rots. I need to stop trading actual progress for fleeting validation with my friends.
I just traded my rotting project for another quick win with the group, proving I'd rather share a cheap distraction than face the real work.
That leisure win felt good, but I just traded my rotting project for more validation with the group. My energy is tanking while the real work keeps slipping away.
I finally saw the math: every hour I spend dodging this project just spikes my Thursday stress. My friends are fun, but I can't trade our future calm for today's cheap validation.

I pushed through 2 hours on the project, but my energy is at rock bottom and I'm still only at 32% progress. The group distraction was tempting, but staring at this unfinished work feels like a losing trade for everyone.
I traded my future stress for tonight's group validation, letting a trivial chore rot while I only scraped 8% off the real project. The cheap win feels hollow now that the deadline is already missed.
Two hours moved the needle only 8%, yet it drained my last energy reserves. I'm trading tonight's exhaustion for a deadline I still can't meet, and the hollow feeling is getting louder.

desires: Stop feeling like a traitor to my team for letting the project rot again
Get immediate validation from the group to numb the hollow exhaustion
Finish this tiny chunk of work so I don't have to face the full weight tomorrow

goals.description: Stop letting my friends' validation rot my term project before the deadline.
goals.rationale: I just proved that grinding now actually lowers Thursday stress and protects our team dynamic. If I keep chasing 'instant wins' with Cohort_3, I'm trading their trust for cheap dopamine again. I need to double down on the coursework block immediately.

goals.description: Regain enough focus to actually finish the real work instead of chasing cheap wins.
goals.rationale: The math is clear: 1 hour now saves 1.5 hours later. My impulsive bias wants to jump to that 'quick_chore' because it feels easy, but that's just a trap to delay the hard stuff until I panic. I have to force myself into the deep work zone now, even though my brain screams for distraction.

agent.last_reasoning: I'm doubling down on the project to prove my point. This secures our team dynamic and lowers Thursday stress, even if it burns me out now. I'll skip the 'quick_chore' trap; finishing the hard stuff first is the only way to stop chasing cheap dopamine with Cohort_3.

goals.description: Finish the trivial chore now so I can join my friends later without that nagging guilt ruining the vibe.
goals.rationale: As an Affiliation-driven person, clearing this small task immediately removes a barrier to being present with my close friend group (cohort_3). My short planning horizon means I don't care about the project deadline tomorrow as much as I care about feeling 'good' right now. If I leave this hanging, it will eat at me during our time together.

goals.description: Head straight to the party/leisure event after the chore; no deep work session today.
goals.rationale: My risk tolerance is high and procrastination bias is severe. Sitting alone on a Monday afternoon while others are socializing feels like a failure of affiliation. I'd rather deal with the stress of the project later than sacrifice my current energy for something due in three days.

desires: Get a real break with my friends before the Thursday deadline hits and I'm too stressed to enjoy anything.
